prose:
The Fukui reactivity analysis of pyridine was completed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level of theory (tier 'standard') in the gas phase, with a charge of 0 and a multiplicity of 1.

The calculation predicts the following reactivity sites:
*   **Most electrophilic atom (most susceptible to nucleophilic attack):** Nitrogen (atom 1), with the largest Fukui(+) index (f+) of 0.4842.
*   **Most nucleophilic atom (most susceptible to electrophilic attack):** Carbon (atom 4), with the largest Fukui(-) index (f-) of 0.1183.

The full Fukui indices and dual descriptor values for each atom are detailed in the table below. These values help quantify the local reactivity of each atom in the molecule. A positive dual descriptor indicates a site is favorable for electrophilic attack, while a negative value indicates it is favorable for nucleophilic attack.

| Atom | Symbol | f+ | f- | f0 | Dual Descriptor |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 1 | N | 0.48423 | 0.06412 | 0.27417 | 0.42010 |
| 2 | C | 0.32447 | 0.00619 | 0.16533 | 0.31828 |
| 3 | C | 0.14032 | 0.11823 | 0.12928 | 0.02209 |
| 4 | C | 0.14040 | 0.11826 | 0.12933 | 0.02213 |
| 5 | C | 0.29963 | 0.11207 | 0.20585 | 0.18756 |
| 6 | C | 0.29948 | 0.11201 | 0.20574 | 0.18747 |
| 7 | H | -0.14151 | 0.08285 | -0.02933 | -0.22436 |
| 8 | H | -0.13368 | 0.09818 | -0.01775 | -0.23186 |
| 9 | H | -0.13366 | 0.09818 | -0.01774 | -0.23184 |
| 10 | H | -0.13982 | 0.09496 | -0.02243 | -0.23478 |
| 11 | H | -0.13985 | 0.09495 | -0.02245 | -0.23479 |

This calculation was performed using the `chemkit` tool, and the results are deemed trustworthy with no warnings reported by the computational engine.
